title: Blechnum angustifolium
taxonomy: Blechnum angustifolium belongs to the family Blechnaceae and is classified as a species of fern. It is a member of the order Polypodiales. The genus Blechnum is a group of vascular plants commonly known as rock ferns or strap ferns. The species Blechnum angustifolium is native to various parts of the world, including New Zealand, Australia, and South America. This plant is characterized by its narrow, linear fronds and is often found growing in rocky or stony areas.
uses: Blechnum angustifolium is a species of fern with various traditional uses, including its application in medicine. It has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat respiratory issues and alleviate coughs. In addition, it is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and preventing infections. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems and to lower fever.
